refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated), incorporates the safety and policy constraints as boundary conditions, and defines the measurable outcome (≥ target over evaluation period) as the verification hook.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Log task cycles, material moved (m³ or ton), elapsed time, and safety/policy compliance events. Calculate productivity = total_material / total_time. Compare against productivity_target. Verify zero safety plan violations and operational policy breaches during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving task cycles (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency ≤ latency_threshold and situational awareness ≥ awareness_threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe operational states.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report productivity metrics (material moved, cycle time, utilization) for post-trial analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic excavator with advanced bucket control and load-sensing hydraulics to minimize cycle time; pair with tele-operated control interface and real-time safety monitoring to achieve target productivity within safety constraints.

DP-B: Deploy autonomous or semi-autonomous vehicle with AI-driven task planning and execution, supported by remote monitoring and safety override capability, to achieve consistent high-productivity cycles independent of operator fatigue.

DP-C: Implement operator-in-the-loop hybrid control: on-site operator for primary task execution with tele-operator backup and safety services overlay to balance productivity, safety, and operational flexibility.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement operator-in-the-loop hybrid control architecture where the on-site operator executes primary earthmoving task cycles (dig, load, transport, dump) with predictable cycle time; tele-operator provides remote monitoring, task coordination, and contingency control; safety services subsystem continuously enforces worksite safety plan constraints and prevents unsafe states. Productivity is achieved through optimized cycle design, operator training, and safety-compliant task scheduling. Telemetry and tasking interfaces enable real-time coordination and safety alert propagation.

potential_sources: Cycle time optimization (FR-NGHE-001.1) is decoupled from teleoperation latency (FR-NGHE-001.2) because on-site operator does not depend on remote latency for primary task execution.
Safety services (FR-NGHE-001.3) is decoupled from productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.4) because safety constraints are enforced independently of logging.
Teleoperation and safety services share Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces but operate on distinct control paths: teleoperation is advisory/backup; safety is hard constraint.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits low coupling because operator-in-the-loop primary execution isolates productivity from remote latency sensitivity. Safety overlay is orthogonal to task execution. Hybrid architecture allows graceful degradation: if tele-operator link fails, on-site operator continues; if safety alert triggers, both operators are constrained.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: On-site operator is trained and motivated to execute cycles at or near target cycle time consistently.
ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan is well-defined and can be encoded into safety services logic.
ASSUMPTION: Network link supports tele-operator monitoring (lower bandwidth than primary control).
RISK: Operator fatigue or skill variance may cause cycle time to drift below target over extended operations.
RISK: Safety services logic may be incomplete or incorrectly specified, leading to unsafe state escape.
RISK: Tele-operator backup may not be available or responsive during contingency, reducing safety margin.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) cycle time target and acceptance tolerance (e.g., ±5%); (2) safety services decision logic (e.g., state machine or rule set); (3) tele-operator response time SLA for contingency takeover; (4) operator training and certification requirements. These refinements will reduce assumption risk and enable clearer verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle (primary task execution), Teleoperation (remote monitoring and backup control), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). The NGHE Solution owns the overall productivity KPI, coordinates task scheduling, and ensures all sub-systems operate within safety and policy constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits telemetry (vehicle state, cycle metrics, safety status) to external monitoring systems and tele-operator console. Includes position, load, cycle time, fuel consumption, safety alerts.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (dig location, load target, dump location, priority) from site manager or tele-operator. Tasking is validated against safety constraints before execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (constraint violation, unsafe state detected, emergency stop) to safety manager and tele-operator. Triggers immediate operator notification and potential control override.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network link to tele-operator console and safety monitoring backend. Supports Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert exchange with acceptable latency (≤ latency_threshold).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI. All sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained within NGHE Solution scope. Diffusion risk is minimal because NGHE Solution is the integration point, not a leaf component. Ownership is clear: system engineer or project manager.

Vehicle module owns cycle time execution (FR-NGHE-001.1) but does not own teleoperation, safety services, or productivity measurement. If productivity KPI is assigned to Vehicle alone, teleoperation and safety services logic would diffuse across multiple modules, violating cohesion. Vehicle is a sub-component, not the top-level container.

Teleoperation module owns remote monitoring and backup control (FR-NGHE-001.2) but does not own vehicle execution, safety services, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI to Teleoperation would create high diffusion because on-site operator and safety services are external. Not suitable as top-level container.

Safety Services module owns constraint enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.3) but does not own vehicle execution, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI to Safety Services would create high diffusion and poor cohesion. Safety Services is a cross-cutting concern, not the top-level container.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (or Project Manager as system integrator). The NGHE Solution is the integration boundary, and ownership must reside at the level that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Individual module owners (vehicle engineer, tele-ops engineer, safety engineer) report to the system engineer for productivity KPI achievement.
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module contains the productivity KPI (FR-NGHE-001) and its sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that implement specific sub-FRs. Productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.4) is a cross-cutting concern owned by NGHE Solution (via logging and reporting infrastructure) but realized by all sub-modules (each emits metrics). This containment is cohesive and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle module is assigned cycle time target (FR-NGHE-001.1) without explicit interface to Teleoperation and Safety Services, vehicle engineer may optimize cycle time in isolation, ignoring safety constraints or tele-operator coordination needs.

RISK: If Safety Services is assigned productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.4), safety engineer may not have visibility into vehicle execution details, leading to incomplete or incorrect productivity logging.

RISK: If Teleoperation is assigned productivity KPI, tele-operator may prioritize remote control responsiveness over on-site operator autonomy, creating unnecessary latency sensitivity and reducing productivity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution module owns the productivity KPI and coordinates sub-modules via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Establish explicit ownership of each sub-FR: Vehicle owns FR-NGHE-001.1 (cycle time), Teleoperation owns FR-NGHE-001.2 (remote monitoring), Safety Services owns FR-NGHE-001.3 (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution owns FR-NGHE-001.4 (productivity measurement and reporting). Document interface contracts (data format, frequency, latency) to ensure sub-modules integrate correctly.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (operator-in-the-loop hybrid control) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve ≥ productivity_target while maintaining safety and policy compliance). The DP specifies a clear solution concept: on-site operator executes primary cycles, tele-operator provides backup, safety services enforces constraints. The DP is implementable and testable. No fundamental mismatch between FR and DP.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): Productivity measurement (FR-NGHE-001.4) requires data collection from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If NGHE Solution does not own a centralized logging/reporting module, metrics may diffuse across sub-modules. Mitigation: establish a dedicated telemetry aggregation service within NGHE Solution.

DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): Safety constraint enforcement (FR-NGHE-001.3) may require coordination between Vehicle (e.g., hydraulic pressure limits) and Safety Services (e.g., geofence checks). If interfaces are not well-defined, logic may diffuse. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert interface contract and safety decision logic upfront.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module provides good containment for the productivity KPI because it is the integration point for all sub-systems. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are logically contained as sub-modules. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimize coupling. Ownership is clear: system engineer owns NGHE Solution; module engineers own sub-modules. No significant containment issues identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period, verify safety plan compliance, and compare against productivity_target. Verification is feasible because productivity is a quantitative metric and safety compliance is observable. Sub-FRs (cycle time, latency, safety enforcement, metrics logging) are also verifiable via instrumentation and testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.3), and NGHE Solution (FR-NGHE-001.4). Each allocation is unambiguous and traceable. No allocation conflicts or gaps identified.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify cycle time target, safety services decision logic, tele-operator response time SLA, and operator training requirements. This will reduce assumption risk and improve verification clarity.
Establish explicit interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. Define data format, frequency, latency, and error handling for each interface.
Define productivity_target value and evaluation period duration. These are critical acceptance criteria and must be quantified before verification.
Establish safety services specification (e.g., state machine, rule set, or formal logic) that encodes worksite safety plan constraints. This will enable verification of FR-NGHE-001.3.
Define operator training and certification requirements to mitigate assumption risk (operator skill variance).

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-001) is decomposable into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) that address distinct aspects of the productivity KPI: cycle time execution, remote monitoring, safety constraint enforcement, and metrics measurement. The selected DP (operator-in-the-loop hybrid control) is sound and implementable, but requires further decomposition to specify cycle time targets, safety logic, and interface contracts. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate as the top-level integration boundary, but sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be further decomposed to realize their respective sub-FRs. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify design details, reduce assumption risk, and enable detailed verification planning.
decision.justification_detail: ZIGZAG_DOWN is appropriate because: (1) the refined FR is non-atomic and explicitly lists four sub-FRs; (2) the selected DP is sound but requires further specification (cycle time target, safety logic, interface contracts); (3) the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is a container module that must be decomposed into sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (4) no fundamental issues with FR-DP-IM alignment have been identified; (5) proceeding to the next level will enable detailed design and verification planning.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving task cycles (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency ≤ latency_threshold and situational awareness ≥ awareness_threshold.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe operational states.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report productivity metrics (material moved, cycle time, utilization) for post-trial analysis.

assumptions: On-site operator is trained and motivated to execute cycles at or near target cycle time consistently.
Worksite safety plan is well-defined and can be encoded into safety services logic.
Network link supports tele-operator monitoring with acceptable latency (≤ latency_threshold).
Productivity_target and evaluation period are defined by customer and are achievable with the selected DP.
Safety criticality (high) does not require formal verification (e.g., SOTIF, functional safety standards) beyond worksite safety plan compliance.
open_risks: Operator fatigue or skill variance may cause cycle time to drift below target over extended operations.
Safety services logic may be incomplete or incorrectly specified, leading to unsafe state escape.
Tele-operator backup may not be available or responsive during contingency, reducing safety margin.
Productivity measurement may be inaccurate if telemetry data is incomplete or corrupted.
Network link may experience latency spikes or outages, affecting tele-operator monitoring and safety alert propagation.
evidence_needed: Quantified productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period duration.
Cycle time target and acceptance tolerance (e.g., ±5%).
Worksite safety plan specification (constraints, hazards, mitigation measures).
Safety services decision logic (state machine, rule set, or formal specification).
Tele-operator response time SLA for contingency takeover.
Network link latency and bandwidth specifications.
Telemetry data format and logging infrastructure design.
Representative operations trial plan (duration, task types, measurement methodology).
